_comment_: WiX 3 Feature manifest for the decoupled (hand-authored) MSI build. Replaces CMake's CPACK_COMPONENT_<NAME>_<KEY> variables (still set in cmake/packaging/windows.cmake for the CPack-WIX path, kept until PR2.c cuts CPack-WIX over). This file plus the install-staging tree are the inputs to scripts/gen_wix_files.py; together they reproduce the Feature, FeatureComponents, and Component/Directory tables byte-equivalently to the WiX 3 MSI that lands in tests/fixtures/msi_golden/wix3_baseline.txt.

_field_notes_.product_feature: Root Feature, parent of every group. Display=1, Attributes=16 (Absent=disallow + AllowAdvertise=no, matches the CPack-emitted ProductFeature row). Directory_=INSTALL_ROOT so cost-finalize anchors install paths under it.
_field_notes_.groups: Each becomes a Feature named CM_G_<name> with parent ProductFeature. Display order observed in the golden: Core=1, Drivers=2, Scripts=4, Tools=6 (CPack skips odd values inside the parent — Scripts and Tools land on 4/6, not 3/4).
_field_notes_.components: Each becomes a Feature named CM_C_<install_component> with parent CM_G_<group> (or ProductFeature for the unspecified group). File-component ComponentRefs into this Feature are auto-derived from the staging tree.
_field_notes_.required: true → emits Absent="disallow" AllowAdvertise="no" which WiX 3 compiles to Attributes=16. Used by CPack for CPACK_COMPONENT_*_REQUIRED.
_field_notes_.depends: CPack uses this to grey-out unselectable child components in the installer UI when a parent is deselected. Not emitted into the MSI tables for runtime — runtime install state is governed by the feature-level Conditions defined below. Retained here as documentation only.
_field_notes_.conditions: Optional list of {level, expression} entries written into the MSI Condition table inside the Feature element. When the expression evaluates true at install time, MSI overrides this feature's Level to the given value (Level=0 means uninstalled, Level=1 means installed). The luminalvgd driver feature gates file staging on the bootstrapper-supplied INSTALL_LUMINALVGD MSI property — without this the VDD custom actions in luminalshine.wxs could fire against files that were never copied. CPack expressed these via <CPackWiXFragment Id="CM_C_sudovda"><Condition>...</Condition></CPackWiXFragment> patch fragments; WiX-native equivalent is a <Condition> child of the <Feature> itself, which gen_wix_files.py emits when this field is present.

components.name: application
components.title: LuminalShine
components.description: LuminalShine main application and required components.

components.name: assets
components.title: Required Assets
components.description: Shaders, default box art, and web UI.

components.name: autostart
components.title: Launch on Startup
components.description: If enabled, launches LuminalShine automatically on system startup.

components.name: luminalvgd
components.title: LuminalVGD (Virtual Display Driver)
components.description: NortheBridge's first-party virtual display driver for per-client virtual displays with exact modes and HDR10. Replaces SudoVDA, which the installer removes when detected.

components.name: firewall
components.title: Add Firewall Exclusions
components.description: Scripts to enable or disable firewall rules.

components.name: audio
components.title: audio-info
components.description: CLI tool providing information about sound devices.

components.name: dxgi
components.title: dxgi-info
components.description: CLI tool providing information about graphics cards and displays.
